title: Adaptive DBSCAN-Enhanced Oversampling for Imbalanced Network Intrusion Detection: A Boundary-Aware Approach
authors: Jingnan Dong, Boran Zhang, Shigen Shen, Jun Liu, Guangxia Xu, Zhiquan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Class imbalance is one of the primary challenges faced by machine learning-based network intrusion detection systems (NIDS), particularly when identifying rare yet high-risk attack types. Traditional oversampling techniques, such as random oversampling and SMOTE, often disrupt the inherent manifold structure of the data and introduce noise near class boundaries, thereby degrading detection performance for minority classes. To address this problem, this paper introduce the ADEO framework-an adaptive DBSCAN-enhanced oversampling method that effectively mitigates these issues by combining deep reinforcement learning with boundary-aware geometric modeling. The framework utilizes a Deep Q-Network (DQN) to automatically optimize DBSCAN parameters, enabling accurate identification of natural clustering structures within heterogeneous intrusion datasets. Building upon this foundation, ADEO constructs precise class boundary functions using methods such as convex hulls, minimum enclosing ellipses, or axis-aligned bounding boxes. It then generates samples within these constrained safe regions, preserving the true topological structure while avoiding noise near decision boundaries. Furthermore, this paper introduces hierarchical class mapping strategies and attack-specific feature engineering to enhance the framework's adaptability to different attack families. Experimental results on the NSL-KDD, UNSW-NB15, and CICIDS2017 benchmark datasets demonstrate that ADEO outperforms mainstream oversampling methods in terms of precision, accuracy, recall, F1 score, FPR, and AUC. Particularly notable improvements are achieved for extreme minority classes like Backdoor and Heartbleed, validating the proposed framework's effectiveness and efficiency in practical NIDS applications.

title: ML-Enabled Dynamic Duplexing for Diverse Traffic Scenarios: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Masoumeh Mokhtari, Petteri Kela, Guillermo Pocovi, Roberto Maldonado, Klaus I. Pedersen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Machine Learning in Communications and Networking
abstract: The emergence of different traffic types with diverse requirements, such as eXtended Reality (XR), in 5G-Advanced and upcoming 6G networks, requires more intelligent duplexing strategies. Sub-band full duplex (SBFD) enables simultaneous uplink (UL) and downlink (DL) transmissions over an unpaired spectrum, thereby providing more frequent UL opportunities. While SBFD has strong potential to improve UL performance without risking DL performance, static radio frame configurations are inherently suboptimal under dynamic traffic conditions. In such cases, dynamic radio frame configurations are required to adapt to varying traffic demands and service requirements and fully realize the benefits of SBFD. This paper proposes an ML-enabled dynamic radio frame selection framework based on a Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) reinforcement learning agent with a two-step bootstrap mechanism. A bootstrapped general model is trained across varied traffic loads and packet sizes to ensure scalability and robustness. System-level simulations in dense urban deployments demonstrate that the bootstrap generalized model achieves good performance, while maintaining adaptability to unseen traffic conditions. Compared with conventional static time-division duplexing (TDD) baseline with “DDDSU”, the proposed scheme increases UL capacity by up to 268% for large-packet file transfer protocol model 3 (FTP3) traffic and 195% for small packets. For XR services, the ML-based solution extends UL capacity by 25%, confirming its effectiveness in supporting latency-sensitive traffic scenarios. The simulation results further indicate that SBFD can deliver a high satisfaction ratio for XR users by ensuring sufficient throughput for cell-edge users.
